should_trigger.text: This handler is impossible to test because it mixes DB queries with business rules. Help me refactor it.
I have a function that both checks eligibility and sends emails. How do I separate the decision from the side effect?
My tests are all mock-heavy and only assert call order. I want to test the actual business logic instead.
Refactor this pricing logic out of the HTTP handler into a pure function.
How do I apply functional core / imperative shell to this Go service that mixes validation with database writes?
This function reads time.Now() and calls an API inside the same business decision. How do I fix this?
I want to extract the state transition logic from this queue consumer into a testable core.
帮我把 handler 里的业务逻辑抽出来，现在 mock 太多了根本测不到核心逻辑。
This domain function accepts *sql.DB directly. How do I introduce boundary DTOs?
My test file has 200 lines of setup just to test one if-else branch. How do I simplify this?
How do I apply the boundaries pattern to this cron job that mixes scheduling with notification logic?
This function both decides what action to take and performs the action immediately. How do I return action values instead?

should_not_trigger.text: Help me design a clean architecture for a new microservice.
Explain what the functional core pattern is.
I need to refactor this function to use generics.
Help me set up database migrations for this project.
Write a script to process CSV files.
How do I configure Docker for this Go app?

near_neighbor.text: I want to reduce the number of mocks in my test suite.
near_neighbor.note: Closely related — mock reduction is a key motivation for boundaries refactor

near_neighbor.text: How do I make this function more testable?
near_neighbor.note: Broad but boundaries refactor is a primary answer

near_neighbor.text: I need to separate concerns in this Go handler.
near_neighbor.note: Boundaries refactor is a concrete approach to separation

near_neighbor.text: How do I refactor this function without breaking existing behavior?
near_neighbor.note: Iterate safely is step 7 of the workflow

near_neighbor.text: My code has too many dependencies injected into every function.
near_neighbor.note: Dependency creep is a signal for boundary extraction

near_neighbor.text: How do I write table-driven tests in Go?
near_neighbor.note: Related but not specific to boundaries refactor — generic Go testing question

near_neighbor.text: Help me refactor this Go code to be more idiomatic.
near_neighbor.note: Too broad — may not involve business rule extraction
